sections.subtitle: Match Overview
sections.text: The upcoming clash between West Ham United and Tottenham Hotspur is set for May 4, 2025, at 13:00 at the London Stadium in London. This Round 35 encounter sees two closely matched teams in the league standings, with Tottenham Hotspur at 16th with 37 points and West Ham United just below at 17th with 36 points. Both teams are desperate for points to climb the table, making this a potentially fiery London derby. The referee, Michael Oliver, averages 3.5 yellow cards per game, which could hint at a physical battle ahead. With both sides struggling for form, this match could hinge on small margins.

sections.subtitle: Recent Head-to-Head History
sections.text: Looking at the last matchup on October 19, 2024, Tottenham Hotspur dominated West Ham United with a convincing 4-1 victory at home. Heung-min Son was the standout player, earning an 8.0 Sofascore rating as the Player of the Match. Over the last 5 meetings between these sides, West Ham have secured 2 wins, Tottenham have 1, and there has been 1 draw, showing a slight edge for the Hammers in recent history. However, head-to-head trends reveal both teams have scored in 7 of the last 8 encounters, suggesting goals at both ends are likely. Additionally, Tottenham have been the first to score in 6 of the last 8 games against West Ham, which could be a psychological advantage.

sections.subtitle: Team Form and Streaks
sections.text: West Ham United are in a worrying slump, currently on a streak of 7 games without a win and 7 games without a clean sheet. They’ve also been the first to concede in 4 of their last 5 matches and have struggled in the first half, losing at halftime in 4 of their last 5 games. Tottenham Hotspur aren’t in much better shape, with no clean sheet in their last 3 away games, though they’ve seen over 2.5 goals in 4 of their last 5 away matches. Both teams have a tendency for both teams to score, with West Ham seeing this in 5 of their last 6 games and Tottenham in 6 of their last 7. This points to an open game with defensive vulnerabilities on display.

sections.subtitle: West Ham United Key Players and Stats
sections.text: West Ham United will rely heavily on Jarrod Bowen, who has scored 9 goals this season against an expected 7.51 xG, showing clinical finishing with a 1.494 overperformance. Bowen is also in form, averaging a 7.14 Sofascore rating over the last 5 matches with 2 goals and 2 assists. Another standout is Mohammed Kudus, ranked 2nd in the league for successful dribbles per game at 3.0 and 5th for fouls drawn per game at 2.1, making him a constant threat on the ball. Tomáš Souček contributes with 8 goals, overperforming his 6.06 xG by 1.943, while James Ward-Prowse has been a consistent performer against Tottenham, averaging a 7.54 Sofascore rating with 3 goals in the last 5 matchups. However, West Ham’s defense is shaky, conceding 58 goals this season (16th worst in the league), despite strong stats like 26.5 clearances per game (5th in the league).

sections.subtitle: Tottenham Hotspur Key Players and Stats
sections.text: Tottenham Hotspur boast attacking threats like Brennan Johnson, who has netted 11 goals against an expected 9.85 xG, showing a slight overperformance of 1.1543. Dejan Kulusevski is another key player, ranked 5th in the league for key passes with 65, contributing 7 goals and 4 assists this season. Pedro Porro has been in excellent form recently, averaging a 7.73 Sofascore rating over the last 5 matches with 2 assists, and his 6 assists for the season highlight his importance from defense. However, Tottenham are missing key players like Heung-min Son, who is ranked 4th in the league for big chances created with 16 and has 7 goals and 9 assists, which could dent their attacking potency. Their goal distribution shows strength late in games, with 17 goals (14.29% of their total) scored between the 76th and 90th minutes.

sections.subtitle: Statistical Insights and Trends
sections.text: West Ham United have scored 39 goals this season, ranking 15th in the league, but overperform their expected goals of 43.27 by 4.27, ranking 6th for goal efficiency. They are strong in headers, with 10 goals from aerial plays (4th in the league), and excel defensively with 8.85 interceptions per game (3rd in the league). However, their 47 big chances missed (7th worst) and 58 goals conceded highlight inefficiencies. Tottenham Hotspur also struggle defensively, conceding big chances with opponents creating 94 against them (16th worst), but their matches often feature goals, with both teams scoring in 6 of their last 7 away games. Head-to-head, the last 5 meetings have seen over 10.5 corners in every game, indicating a high likelihood of set-piece action.

sections.subtitle: Missing Players and Tactical Impact
sections.text: West Ham United will miss Michail Antonio, a forward with a notable league rank of 3rd for percentage of long balls in total passes at 11%, which could affect their direct play style. Another absence, Crysencio Summerville, deprives them of midfield creativity with 1 goal and 1 assist this season. Tottenham Hotspur face significant losses with James Maddison out, who has 9 goals and 7 assists with a 7.3 Sofascore rating, alongside Dominic Solanke (8 goals) and Heung-min Son, whose absence could blunt their attack. These absences might force Tottenham to rely more on players like Brennan Johnson and Dejan Kulusevski for inspiration, while West Ham will need Jarrod Bowen and Mohammed Kudus to step up.
